title: THE ANTHROPO-SUFI DRAMA PERFORMANCE: THE ANTHROPOLOGICAL DIMENSION AND IMAGE STYLIZATION
author: Frik Mohamed Amir
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This article focuses on a new methodology for transformation of a specific aspect of the North African heritage (specifically Tunisia, Algeria and Morocco)  –  Sufi Hadra Ceremony, into a theatrical performance, known as the Anthropo-Sufi Drama Performance. This type of performance reconciles with the Western theatrical stage without Aristotelian domination in its final form. The principles and steps of this transformation process have been structured on the basis of combination of anthropological and theatrical spheres. The research was developed by the application of analytical method, cultural-historical analysis, formal and stylistic analysis, and comparative analysis, as well as experimental and descriptive methods used to define the main features of an Anthropo-Sufi Drama Performance
